stories.title: Iran war hinders the flow of U.N. aid through the Gulf to communities in need
stories.description: It's not just oil tankers stranded near the Strait of Hormuz. U.N. aid shipments are also hindered by the war in Iran.

stories.title: With strikes above and crackdowns on the ground, Iranians describe life under siege
stories.description: A state-imposed internet blackout has obscured the reality of life in Iran as the war rages on. Those fleeing through neighboring countries share a rare glimpse of what life is like in Iran.

stories.title: Author Scott Anderson on Iran's power structure after death of top security official
stories.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with journalist and author Scott Anderson about Iran's power structure after the killing of Ali Larijani, the head of Iran's Supreme National Security Council.

stories.title: War can't entirely eliminate Iran's nuclear program, the U.N. atomic energy chief says
stories.description: International Atomic Energy Agency head Rafael Grossi said Iran's nuclear program is heavily damaged, "but the material will still be there and the enrichment capacities will be there."

stories.title: Cheap drones are reshaping modern warfare — and catching the U.S. off guard
stories.description: As Operation Epic Fury enters its third week, relentless attacks by cheap Iranian drones are being fended off by multi-million-dollar U.S. interceptors. How long can the math hold up?

stories.title: Morning news brief
stories.description: Iranians fleeing their country share rare glimpse of what life is like amid war, Trump floats idea of "taking" Cuba, the Federal Reserve is expected to hold its benchmark interest rate steady.

stories.title: Once the airstrikes stop, Iran's nuclear threat leaves no easy endgame
stories.description: Even after airstrikes end, Iran's nuclear threat looms and diplomacy may be too late.

stories.title: 'Very difficult to stop': BBC visits scene of Iran cluster bomb strike on Israel
stories.description: An elderly couple was killed after a bomb flew into their apartment in central Israel and exploded.

stories.title: Why the Strait of Hormuz matters so much in the Iran war
stories.description: Iran says it will "set fire" to ships trying to sail through the world's most vital oil transit point.

stories.title: Iran's intelligence minister Esmail Khatib killed in air strike
stories.description: Israel said it had killed Khatib, whose death was confirmed by Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian on Wednesday.

stories.title: Surge in US gas prices deepens political peril for Trump over Iran
stories.description: The odds of a quick resolution to the war appear to be dwindling by the day, and the political stakes for the president are only growing.

stories.title: Wary allies show there's no quick fix to Trump's Iran crisis
stories.description: European leaders are hesitant to help Trump secure the Strait of Hormuz, but they know inaction on the Iran war is not really an option.

stories.title: Israel says 'limited' ground operations under way in Lebanon
stories.description: Troops are targeting "Hezbollah strongholds" in south Lebanon, where strikes reportedly killed nine people on Monday.

stories.title: How might the UK support shipping in the Strait of Hormuz?
stories.description: Ben Chu from BBC Verify has been looking at what role the Royal Navy could play in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Who wants what from the Iran war?
stories.description: Most people, although not everybody, want this war to end as quickly as possible. But on what terms?

stories.title: Iran taking steps to prevent anti-establishment protests, Tehran residents tell BBC
stories.description: Residents describe new security checkpoints around the capital, where they say people are stopped and searched.

stories.title: Iran hits key UAE oil port and Dubai airport
stories.description: The port of Fujairah plays a crucial role in helping keep global supplies moving when the Strait of Hormuz is blocked.

stories.title: What Iranians are being told about the war
stories.description: Iranian state media's coverage of the war includes inflations of enemy casualties and digital manipulation intended to glorify Iran.

stories.title: Iranian footballer says 'everything will be fine' as she trains with Australian team
stories.description: Several players were granted Australian visas after concerns the team would face repercussions at home.

stories.title: Iran captain latest footballer to drop asylum bid, Iranian state media says
stories.description: Five of the seven members of the women's football delegation who originally sought to stay in Australia have now chosen to return home.

stories.title: US names six crew killed in refuelling plane crash in Iraq
stories.description: The aircraft was on a combat mission as part of ongoing US operations against Iran when it crashed in western Iraq on Thursday.

stories.title: BBC visits aftermath of Israeli strike on Lebanon that killed family as IDF targets Hezbollah
stories.description: A relative tells BBC those killed were civilians and not Hezbollah operatives, but the Israel Defense Forces says it was targeting "terrorist infrastructure".

stories.title: Israel kills Iran’s intel chief as both sides ratchet up attacks on energy facilities
stories.description: Iran suffered major blows on Wednesday with the killing of its intelligence minister, Esmail Khatib, in an Israeli strike and an attack on the South Pars natural gas field, one of the world's largest gas resources and a pillar of the country's energy supplies. Tehran vowed retali...

stories.title: US seeks to curb soaring petrol prices
stories.description: The Trump administration is taking steps to curb rising energy costs, as it continues to wage war in the Middle East. The US is easing sanctions on Venezuela, and suspending a century-old shipping law, in an effort to get more oil supplies into the global market. Oil prices conti...

stories.title: 'Acute risk' that Iraq could become once again victim of internal conflict as Iran war progresses
stories.description: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Yinka Oyetade, Zaid Al-Ali, Visiting Fellow at Princeton University and Former UN Advisor in Iraq, explains that until the war in Iran broke out, Iraq had been able to "straddle between US-aligned interests and Iranian-aligned interests" but there's an "...

stories.title: Iran did not try to rebuild enrichment after June 2025 attack, US intel chief says
stories.description: Iran made no effort to rebuild uranium enrichment after its capabilities were destroyed in a June 2025 US-Israeli attack, US intelligence chief Tulsi Gabbard testified Wednesday, contradicting President Donald Trump's justifications for his ongoing war. FRANCE 24's Fraser Jackson...

stories.title: Both sides in Iran war ratchet up attacks on energy facilities, as oil prices surge
stories.description: Saudi Arabia intercepted and destroyed four ballistic missiles that were launched ​toward Riyadh on Wednesday, the Defence Ministry said, just ahead of a meeting of regional and Islamic foreign ministers in the Saudi capital. FRANCE 24's Hoda Abdel-Hamid reports from Doha.

stories.title: Trump's dire strait: No strategy, no endgame, no allied support?
stories.description: Today in the Middle East, Day 19 of the war, there have been extensive air strikes, drone attacks and missiles launched. And fall-out is spreading far beyond the so called theatre of war. And the funeral of Ali Larijani, Iran's de facto leader, key advisor to the Ayatollah, the h...

stories.title: 'Not a one-person regime': Why Iran's Islamic Republic is so hard to topple
stories.description: Since the killing of Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei on February 28, US and Israeli strikes have decimated the regime's top ranks, from the commander of the Revolutionary Guards, to the country's security and intelligence chiefs. But even as the heads of the Iranian ...

stories.title: Elisa Catalano Ewers: Israeli leadership moves to destabilise regime’s leadership structure
stories.description: Delano D'Souza is pleased to welcome Elisa Catalano Ewers, Senior Fellow for Middle East Studies at the Council on Foreign Relations. Our guest illustrates how Israeli envisions destroying the Iranian regime from within. Their approach is not one of immediate collapse, but of cal...

stories.title: Soler i Lecha: Foreign policy of Pedro Sánchez 'widely supported by the Spanish population'
stories.description: As Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelensky meets with European leaders, including Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez, Eve Irvine is pleased to welcome Eduard Soler i Lecha, Associate Professor of International Relations at the Universitat Autònoma de Barcelona and Senior Researc...

stories.title: Tyre emptied as Israel orders evacuation across southern Lebanon
stories.description: At war with Iran-backed Hezbollah, Israel on Wednesday has called on everyone living south of the Zahrani River to evacuate immediately. It is an area comprising 14 percent of Lebanon's territory, and is highlighted in red in the maps posted by the Israeli military. FRANCE 24's C...

stories.title: Crowds gather for funeral of top security official Ali Larijani
stories.description: He was the late Ayatollah Ali #Khamenei's closest adviser. Ali #Larijani was killed in an overnight strike, nearly a month after the death of the supreme leader.

stories.title: 'Decapitation' strategy: Israel seeks to destroy Iran command and control
stories.description: Israel said on Wednesday its forces had killed another top Iranian official, Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib, and said its military was authorised to kill any senior figure of the Islamic republic in its sights. The announcement, the day after Iranian security chief Ali Larij...

stories.title: South Lebanon residents flee death and destruction
stories.description: Residents of Lebanon southern areas bombarded in the Israel-Hezbollah war poured into the ancient city of Sidon in search of safety. At war with Iran-backed Hezbollah, Israel has called on everyone living south of the Zahrani River to evacuate immediately. FRANCE 24's Claire Pacc...

stories.title: Trump pressures media on Iran war with claims of 'treason'
stories.description: US President Donald Trump and his allies are attacking news organisations for their coverage of the war on Iran, a conflict the administration says is going well for the United States but is unpopular with the public and has plunged the Middle East into chaos. Trump accused unspe...

stories.title: Tens of millions more in acute hunger if Mideast war drags on, UN warns
stories.description: An extra 45 million people could face acute hunger if the Mideast war carried on through June, swelling the number worldwide to a "terrible" high, the United Nations warned Tuesday. The war, now in its third week, has killed hundreds and seen Iran launch retaliatory strikes again...

stories.title: Iranians burn Trump, Netanyahu effigies at warship victim rally
stories.description: Iranians in Tehran burned effigies of Israeli PM Netanyahu and US President Trump.

stories.title: US Fed keeps interest rates steady amid economic, geopolitical uncertainty
stories.description: The US Federal Reserve keeps rates steady at 3.5–3.75 percent amidst a cooling labour market and Iran pressures.

stories.title: Oil prices surge after Israeli strike on Iran’s South Pars gasfield
stories.description: There are fears that a period of elevated oil and gas prices could trigger a damaging wave of global inflation.

stories.title: Top US spy accused of omitting Iran intel that contradicts Trump
stories.description: Top US spy was accused of altering testimony on Iran, omitting details that contradict Trump’s threat claims.

stories.title: Israel attacks Iran’s major gas field in new escalation
stories.description: Israel has struck facilities belonging to the world's largest natural gas field in Iran, marking a sharp escalation.

stories.title: US intel chief Gabbard says Iran was not rebuilding enrichment prior to war
stories.description: Gabbard's testimony contradicts one of several justifications Trump has given for launching war with Iran.

stories.title: Qatar says Iran missile attack sparks fire, causes damage at gas facility
stories.description: Qatar's Foreign Ministry strongly condemns attack that caused 'extensive damage' at the Ras Laffan complex.

stories.title: Could the Iran war trigger a global recession?
stories.description: Energy prices are surging as the Iran war disrupts supply, raising risks for the US, China and Europe.

stories.title: Who will answer for the Iranian schoolchildren killed in Minab?
stories.description: An investigation finds the Minab school strike may have been deliberate. Who can hold the US accountable?

stories.title: Iranian women’s football team return to Iran
stories.description: The Iranian national women's football team has returned to Iran, including several players who withdrew asylum requests.

stories.title: Gunshots disperse crowd during Persian New Year festivities in Tehran
stories.description: A crowd celebrating Chaharshanbe Suri in Tehran were forced to flee after gunshots disrupted their event.

stories.title: How Los Angeles’s Iranian diaspora is confronting the US war on Iran
stories.description: Residents are divided between supporting and opposing the US and Israeli war against Iran, amid fears for loved ones.

stories.title: Iran threatens to strike Gulf energy facilities after South Pars attack
stories.description: Qatar foreign ministry spokesman slams Israeli attack on Iran's offshore gasfield as 'dangerous, irresponsible step'.

stories.title: How the killing of Iran’s Ali Larijani could impact the war
stories.description: How will the killing of Ali Larijani impact Iran’s leadership and the country’s security?

stories.title: How Dubai and UAE are being affected by Iran attacks and US-Israeli war
stories.description: How Dubai and UAE are being affected by Iran attacks and US-Israeli war

stories.title: ‘Free France’: Macron reveals name of Europe’s largest warship
stories.description: President Emmanuel Macron on Wednesday announced that France’s next nuclear-powered aircraft carrier will be called “France Libre” (Free France), as the country looks to reinforce its status as a major maritime power. Once completed, the warship, which is set to replace the count...

stories.title: Anthropic AI an ‘unacceptable risk’ to military, US govt says
stories.description: Artificial intelligence company Anthropic posed an “unacceptable risk” to military supply chains, the US government insisted on Tuesday, as it defends against the tech firm’s challenge to a designation as dangerous. Anthropic’s Claude AI model has been in the spotlight in recent ...

stories.title: Saudi Arabia to celebrate Eidul Fitr on Friday
stories.description: Saudi Arabia announced that Eidul Fitr would be celebrated on Friday, according to a statement published by state media. “The Supreme Court has decided that tomorrow, Thursday, is the completion of the 30th day of the month of Ramazan, and that Friday is the day of the Blessed Ei...

stories.title: Press review: Europe seeks to block US-Russia thaw as Russia eyes role in US-Iran talks
stories.description: Top stories from the Russian press on Friday, June 6th

stories.title: Israel to continue striking Beirut until Lebanon disarms Hezbollah — defense minister
stories.description: "Agreements must be honored and the Lebanese government does not do what is required, Israel will continue to act, and with great force," Israel Katz said

stories.title: Orban sees EU bracing for another decade of countering Russia amid proxy war in Ukraine
stories.description: Commenting on the Ukraine crisis, the Hungarian PM said that Brussels viewed Ukraine as "the first line of European defense" where pan-European interests were currently being defended

stories.title: Israel strikes Hezbollah targets in Beirut suburbs — IDF
stories.description: Prior to that, the Israeli army issued a warning for this neighborhood, telling residents to leave their homes immediately

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
